title: Ensure MFA is enabled for all users
description: Verifies that multi-factor authentication is enforced for every user account in the Entra ID tenant via Security Defaults or Conditional Access.
rationale: MFA dramatically reduces the risk of account compromise from phishing and credential theft.
impact: Users must register a second factor before sign-in.
audit_procedure: Azure Portal → Microsoft Entra ID → Security → Conditional Access → review MFA policies
remediation_summary: Enable Security Defaults or create a Conditional Access policy requiring MFA for all users.

title: Ensure MFA is enabled for all privileged users
description: Checks that every user holding a privileged directory role has MFA registered and enforced.
rationale: Privileged accounts are the highest-value targets; compromise leads to tenant-wide impact.
impact: Admins must complete MFA on every privileged sign-in.
audit_procedure: Azure Portal → Entra ID → Users → Per-user MFA; verify all admins have MFA enforced
remediation_summary: Assign MFA to all accounts with Global Admin, Privileged Role Admin, or similar roles.

title: Ensure guest users are reviewed regularly
description: Verifies that access reviews or a periodic process exists to audit guest (B2B) accounts in the tenant.
rationale: Stale guest accounts accumulate and become attack vectors if not pruned.
impact: Guest access may be removed periodically requiring re-invitation.
audit_procedure: Azure Portal → Entra ID → Identity Governance → Access reviews → verify review exists for guests
remediation_summary: Create an access review for All guests recurring quarterly.

title: Ensure no custom subscription owner roles exist
description: Checks that no custom RBAC role assignable at subscription scope grants the equivalent of Owner.
rationale: Custom owner roles bypass audit and PIM controls applied to the built-in Owner role.
impact: Users holding custom owner roles must be reassigned to built-in roles.
audit_procedure: Azure Portal → Subscriptions → Access control (IAM) → Roles → filter Custom
remediation_summary: Remove any custom role with Actions=* at subscription scope.

title: Ensure subscription owners have MFA
description: Verifies that every principal assigned the Owner role on a subscription has MFA enforced.
rationale: Subscription owners can destroy or exfiltrate any resource in scope; MFA is essential.
impact: Owners must complete MFA per session.
audit_procedure: Azure Portal → Subscriptions → Access control → Role assignments → Owner; cross-check Entra MFA state
remediation_summary: Enforce Conditional Access MFA policy targeting subscription owner group.

title: Ensure external invitations are restricted
description: Checks that only admins or designated roles can invite guests to the tenant.
rationale: Unrestricted invitation allows unvetted external identities to receive access.
impact: End users lose the ability to invite external collaborators without request.
audit_procedure: Azure Portal → Entra ID → External Identities → External collaboration settings
remediation_summary: Set Guest invite settings to 'Only users assigned to specific admin roles'.

title: Ensure no service principal credentials are expired
description: Checks that no service principal has expired certificate or secret credentials still associated.
rationale: Expired credentials indicate rotation failures and can leave abandoned secrets in source control.
impact: Expired entries must be removed and replaced with active credentials.
audit_procedure: Azure Portal → Entra ID → App registrations → Certificates & secrets → review expirations
remediation_summary: Delete expired secrets/certs and rotate via the owning app pipeline.

title: Ensure legacy authentication is blocked
description: Verifies a Conditional Access policy blocks legacy (non-MFA-capable) authentication protocols.
rationale: Legacy auth bypasses MFA and is the top vector for password spray attacks.
impact: Clients using POP/IMAP/SMTP basic auth will stop working.
audit_procedure: Azure Portal → Entra ID → Conditional Access → verify policy blocks Other clients / Legacy auth
remediation_summary: Create CAP targeting 'Other clients' with Block access.

title: Ensure password reset notification is enabled
description: Checks that users are notified when their password is reset via self-service.
rationale: Notification alerts legitimate users to unauthorized password reset attempts.
impact: Users receive email on SSPR events.
audit_procedure: Azure Portal → Entra ID → Password reset → Notifications → 'Notify users on password resets' = Yes
remediation_summary: Set Notify users on password resets to Yes.

title: Ensure no more than 3 subscription owners
description: Verifies subscription owner count is bounded to reduce blast radius.
rationale: Fewer owners reduces the number of high-value credentials an attacker can target.
impact: Additional owners must be downgraded to Contributor or similar.
audit_procedure: Azure Portal → Subscriptions → Access control → Role assignments → count Owners
remediation_summary: Remove excess owners; prefer PIM eligible assignments.

title: Ensure no disabled accounts have role assignments
description: Checks that no Entra-disabled user retains active RBAC or directory role assignments.
rationale: Disabled accounts with assignments can be re-enabled and abused without detection.
impact: Role assignments must be cleaned up before account disable.
audit_procedure: Azure Portal → Entra ID → Users → filter Disabled → cross-check role assignments
remediation_summary: Remove all RBAC assignments for disabled accounts.

title: Ensure no guest users have privileged roles
description: Verifies that no guest (B2B) account holds a privileged directory or RBAC role.
rationale: External identities holding admin access broaden the trust boundary beyond the organization.
impact: Guests with admin roles must be replaced with member accounts.
audit_procedure: Azure Portal → Entra ID → Roles and administrators → review members for userType=Guest
remediation_summary: Remove guest accounts from privileged roles; convert to member if needed.

title: Ensure access reviews exist for privileged roles
description: Checks that Entra PIM access reviews are configured on all privileged directory roles.
rationale: Periodic reviews detect and remove stale or inappropriate privilege.
impact: Role holders may be deactivated during review if they fail to attest.
audit_procedure: Azure Portal → Entra ID → Identity Governance → Privileged Access → Access reviews
remediation_summary: Create recurring access reviews on each privileged role.

title: Ensure PIM is configured for privileged role activation
description: Verifies that privileged roles are assigned as PIM-eligible rather than permanent.
rationale: Just-in-time activation removes standing privilege and reduces credential exposure.
impact: Admins must activate roles each session via PIM.
audit_procedure: Azure Portal → Entra ID → PIM → Azure AD roles → Assignments → verify Eligible > Active
remediation_summary: Convert permanent assignments to PIM eligible with activation MFA.

title: Ensure App Registrations have no high-privilege Graph permissions
description: Checks that no App Registration is granted high-risk Graph application permissions such as Directory.ReadWrite.All without justification.
rationale: Consented application-level permissions bypass user context and are a common persistence vector.
impact: Apps needing these permissions must go through a review workflow.
audit_procedure: Azure Portal → Entra ID → App registrations → API permissions → filter Application type
remediation_summary: Revoke unnecessary Graph permissions; require admin consent workflow.

title: Ensure Defender for Servers plan is set to Standard
description: Verifies the Microsoft Defender for Servers plan is enabled at Standard tier on the subscription.
rationale: Defender for Servers provides EDR, vulnerability scanning, and JIT VM access.
impact: Incurs per-node cost; improves workload security posture.
audit_procedure: Azure Portal → Microsoft Defender for Cloud → Environment settings → Plans → Servers
remediation_summary: Enable Defender for Servers Plan 2 on each subscription.

title: Ensure Defender for App Service plan is set to Standard
description: Verifies Defender for App Service is enabled at Standard tier.
rationale: Defender monitors App Service for abuse, cryptomining, and webshell upload.
audit_procedure: Azure Portal → Defender for Cloud → Environment settings → Plans → App Service
remediation_summary: Toggle Defender for App Service to On.

title: Ensure Defender for SQL plan is set to Standard
description: Verifies Defender for SQL (Databases) is enabled Standard across servers.
rationale: Provides SQL threat detection and vulnerability assessment.
audit_procedure: Azure Portal → Defender for Cloud → Environment settings → Plans → Databases
remediation_summary: Enable Defender for SQL on subscription plans.

title: Ensure Defender for Storage plan is set to Standard
description: Verifies Defender for Storage is enabled at Standard tier.
rationale: Detects malicious uploads, anonymous access anomalies, and data exfiltration.
audit_procedure: Azure Portal → Defender for Cloud → Environment settings → Plans → Storage
remediation_summary: Enable Defender for Storage on subscription.

title: Ensure Defender for Containers plan is set to Standard
description: Verifies Defender for Containers is enabled protecting AKS and ACR.
rationale: Runtime protection, image scanning, and Kubernetes audit log analytics.
audit_procedure: Azure Portal → Defender for Cloud → Environment settings → Plans → Containers
remediation_summary: Enable Defender for Containers.

title: Ensure Defender for Key Vault plan is set to Standard
description: Verifies Defender for Key Vault is enabled at Standard tier.
rationale: Detects anomalous secret access and suspicious enumeration.
audit_procedure: Azure Portal → Defender for Cloud → Environment settings → Plans → Key Vault
remediation_summary: Enable Defender for Key Vault.

title: Ensure Defender for DNS plan is set to Standard
description: Verifies Defender for DNS is enabled to detect malicious domain resolution.
rationale: Detects DNS tunneling, C2, and cryptomining domains.
audit_procedure: Azure Portal → Defender for Cloud → Environment settings → Plans → DNS
remediation_summary: Enable Defender for DNS on subscription.

title: Ensure Defender for ARM plan is set to Standard
description: Verifies Defender for Resource Manager is enabled detecting control-plane attacks.
rationale: Alerts on suspicious ARM operations such as mass disable or impossible travel.
audit_procedure: Azure Portal → Defender for Cloud → Environment settings → Plans → Resource Manager
remediation_summary: Enable Defender for Resource Manager.

title: Ensure secure transfer is required for storage accounts
description: Verifies that storage accounts require HTTPS (supportsHttpsTrafficOnly=true).
rationale: Prevents plaintext transfer of data over HTTP.
impact: Legacy clients using HTTP will break.
audit_procedure: Azure Portal → Storage account → Configuration → Secure transfer required = Enabled
remediation_summary: Set supportsHttpsTrafficOnly to true via Terraform or CLI.

title: Ensure infrastructure encryption is enabled for storage accounts
description: Checks that storage accounts have double-layer platform-managed encryption enabled.
rationale: Provides defense-in-depth against encryption-layer compromise.
impact: Cannot be disabled post-creation; requires new account.
audit_procedure: Azure Portal → Storage account → Encryption → Infrastructure encryption = Enabled
remediation_summary: Recreate storage account with requireInfrastructureEncryption=true.

title: Ensure storage accounts disallow public blob access
description: Verifies allowBlobPublicAccess is false on all storage accounts.
rationale: Public blob access is a leading cause of cloud data leaks.
impact: Applications relying on anonymous blobs must adopt SAS or AAD auth.
audit_procedure: Azure Portal → Storage account → Configuration → Allow Blob public access = Disabled
remediation_summary: Set allow_blob_public_access=false.

title: Ensure default action on storage account network rules is set to Deny
description: Checks that networkRuleSet.defaultAction is Deny requiring an explicit allow.
rationale: Denies network access by default enforcing zero trust posture.
impact: Must configure explicit allow lists or private endpoints before enabling.
audit_procedure: Azure Portal → Storage account → Networking → Public network access = Enabled from selected networks
remediation_summary: Set network_rules.default_action=Deny.

title: Ensure private endpoint is used on storage accounts
description: Verifies a Private Endpoint connection exists for each storage account.
rationale: Private endpoints keep storage traffic on the Azure backbone, eliminating public exposure.
audit_procedure: Azure Portal → Storage account → Networking → Private endpoint connections
remediation_summary: Create private endpoint in workload VNet; update DNS zone.

title: Ensure blob soft delete is enabled
description: Checks that blob soft delete retention is configured for at least 7 days.
rationale: Enables recovery from accidental or malicious deletion of blobs.
impact: Minor storage cost for retained deleted data.
audit_procedure: Azure Portal → Storage account → Data protection → Soft delete for blobs
remediation_summary: Enable delete_retention_policy with retention_days>=7.

title: Ensure container soft delete is enabled
description: Checks that container-level soft delete is enabled for 7+ days.
rationale: Recovers deleted containers that would otherwise purge all contained blobs.
impact: Minor cost; undelete window covers mistakes.
audit_procedure: Azure Portal → Storage account → Data protection → Soft delete for containers
remediation_summary: Enable container_delete_retention_policy.

title: Ensure storage logging is enabled for queue and table service
description: Verifies classic diagnostic logs (Read/Write/Delete) are enabled on queue and table endpoints.
rationale: Audit trail is required for incident response on storage data plane.
audit_procedure: Azure Portal → Storage account → Diagnostic settings (classic) → Queue and Table
remediation_summary: Enable logging for queue and table services.

title: Ensure SQL server auditing is enabled
description: Checks that Azure SQL server auditing is enabled writing to a Log Analytics workspace or storage.
rationale: Provides forensic trail of SQL DML/DDL for detection and investigation.
audit_procedure: Azure Portal → SQL server → Auditing → State=Enabled
remediation_summary: Enable mssql_server_extended_auditing_policy.

title: Ensure SQL transparent data encryption is enabled
description: Verifies TDE is enabled on each Azure SQL database.
rationale: Protects data at rest from storage-layer compromise.
impact: Enabled by default on new databases.
audit_procedure: Azure Portal → SQL database → Transparent data encryption = Enabled
remediation_summary: Set mssql_database_transparent_data_encryption_enabled=true.

title: Ensure SQL threat detection is enabled
description: Verifies Defender for SQL threat protection is enabled at server level.
rationale: Alerts on SQL injection, anomalous access, and credential compromise.
audit_procedure: Azure Portal → SQL server → Microsoft Defender for SQL
remediation_summary: Enable mssql_server_security_alert_policy.

title: Ensure SQL public network access is disabled
description: Checks that Azure SQL public network access is Disabled.
rationale: Ensures connections arrive only through private endpoints.
impact: Requires private endpoint for client access.
audit_procedure: Azure Portal → SQL server → Networking → Public network access = Disable
remediation_summary: Set public_network_access_enabled=false.

title: Ensure PostgreSQL SSL enforcement is enabled
description: Verifies ssl_enforcement is Enabled on Azure Database for PostgreSQL.
rationale: Prevents downgrade attacks and plaintext credential capture.
audit_procedure: Azure Portal → PostgreSQL server → Connection security → Enforce SSL = Enabled
remediation_summary: Set ssl_enforcement_enabled=true.

title: Ensure MySQL SSL enforcement is enabled
description: Verifies ssl_enforcement is Enabled on Azure Database for MySQL.
rationale: Encrypts client connections and prevents credential interception.
audit_procedure: Azure Portal → MySQL server → Connection security → Enforce SSL = Enabled
remediation_summary: Set ssl_enforcement_enabled=true on mysql_server.

title: Ensure diagnostic setting exists for Azure subscription
description: Verifies a subscription-level diagnostic setting exports activity logs to Log Analytics or storage.
rationale: Activity log retention is the foundation of audit and detection.
audit_procedure: Azure Portal → Monitor → Activity log → Export activity logs
remediation_summary: Create monitor_diagnostic_setting at subscription scope.

title: Ensure activity log retention is at least 365 days
description: Checks that activity log exports retain data for at least 365 days.
rationale: Long retention supports forensic investigations months after compromise.
audit_procedure: Azure Portal → Monitor → Diagnostic settings → Retention (days) >= 365
remediation_summary: Set retention_policy.days>=365 on archive storage.

title: Ensure activity log alert exists for policy assignment changes
description: Verifies an activity log alert is configured for Microsoft.Authorization/policyAssignments/write.
rationale: Detects unauthorized policy modification that could remove guardrails.
audit_procedure: Azure Portal → Monitor → Alerts → Alert rules → filter Create policy assignment
remediation_summary: Create activity log alert on policyAssignments write/delete.

title: Ensure activity log alert exists for NSG changes
description: Checks for activity log alerts on networkSecurityGroups write/delete operations.
rationale: Network rule changes are a common lateral-movement precursor.
audit_procedure: Azure Portal → Monitor → Alerts → review NSG change alerts
remediation_summary: Create activity log alert on Microsoft.Network/networkSecurityGroups/write.

title: Ensure activity log alert exists for security solution changes
description: Verifies alerts on Microsoft.Security/securitySolutions write/delete operations.
rationale: Attackers commonly disable Defender components before action.
audit_procedure: Azure Portal → Monitor → Alerts → security solutions rules
remediation_summary: Create activity log alert on securitySolutions changes.

title: Ensure activity log alert exists for SQL firewall changes
description: Checks for activity log alerts on Microsoft.Sql/servers/firewallRules write/delete.
rationale: Firewall rule changes may expose SQL to the internet.
audit_procedure: Azure Portal → Monitor → Alerts → SQL firewall rules alerts
remediation_summary: Create activity log alert on Sql firewallRules write.

title: Ensure no NSG allows SSH from internet
description: Verifies no NSG rule permits inbound TCP/22 from 0.0.0.0/0 or Any.
rationale: Internet-exposed SSH is continuously scanned and brute-forced.
impact: Admin access must move to Bastion or JIT.
audit_procedure: Azure Portal → Network security groups → Inbound rules → filter port 22 source Any
remediation_summary: Remove or restrict the NSG rule; use Azure Bastion.

title: Ensure no NSG allows RDP from internet
description: Verifies no NSG rule permits inbound TCP/3389 from 0.0.0.0/0 or Any.
rationale: Internet-exposed RDP is a leading ransomware entry vector.
impact: Remote admins must use Bastion or JIT VM access.
audit_procedure: Azure Portal → NSG → Inbound rules → filter port 3389 source Any
remediation_summary: Remove rule or scope source to known IPs; prefer Bastion.

title: Ensure no NSG allows UDP services from internet
description: Checks that no NSG rule permits unrestricted inbound UDP from Any source.
rationale: Open UDP services are frequently abused for amplification attacks.
impact: Legitimate UDP traffic must be scoped.
audit_procedure: Azure Portal → NSG → Inbound rules → filter protocol UDP source Any
remediation_summary: Constrain UDP inbound rules to specific source ranges.

title: Ensure Network Watcher is enabled in all regions
description: Verifies a Network Watcher instance exists in every subscription region.
rationale: Network Watcher is prerequisite for packet capture, NSG flow logs, and connectivity diagnostics.
audit_procedure: Azure Portal → Network Watcher → Overview → verify per-region instance
remediation_summary: Create network_watcher in each active region.

title: Ensure NSG flow logs are enabled
description: Checks that each NSG has flow logs configured to a storage account.
rationale: Flow logs provide evidence of denied and allowed connections for detection.
audit_procedure: Azure Portal → Network Watcher → NSG flow logs
remediation_summary: Enable network_watcher_flow_log on each NSG.

title: Ensure no public IP on management VMs
description: Verifies VMs tagged or roled as management do not have public IP addresses attached.
rationale: Public management IPs expose the attack surface and bypass bastion controls.
audit_procedure: Azure Portal → Virtual machines → filter Public IP assigned
remediation_summary: Detach public IP; use Azure Bastion for admin access.

title: Ensure VM endpoint protection is installed
description: Checks that Microsoft Antimalware or Defender for Endpoint is deployed on Windows/Linux VMs.
rationale: Endpoint protection detects and blocks on-host malware.
audit_procedure: Azure Portal → Microsoft Defender for Cloud → Recommendations → Endpoint protection
remediation_summary: Deploy Microsoft Antimalware extension or onboard to MDE.

title: Ensure VM OS disk is encrypted
description: Verifies that VM OS disks have Azure Disk Encryption or platform-managed encryption at host enabled.
rationale: Protects OS volume contents if physical media is compromised.
impact: ADE requires Key Vault; minor boot time impact.
audit_procedure: Azure Portal → Virtual machine → Disks → Encryption
remediation_summary: Enable encryption_at_host or Azure Disk Encryption.

title: Ensure VM data disks are encrypted
description: Checks that all attached managed data disks are encrypted with CMK or platform keys.
rationale: Data disks often hold sensitive databases that require encryption at rest.
audit_procedure: Azure Portal → VM → Disks → Data disks → Encryption type
remediation_summary: Create disks with disk_encryption_set_id.

title: Ensure vulnerability assessment solution is installed on VMs
description: Verifies Defender for Cloud built-in vuln assessment or Qualys agent is deployed.
rationale: Continuous vulnerability scanning is the basis for patch prioritization.
audit_procedure: Azure Portal → Defender for Cloud → Recommendations → Vulnerabilities in VMs
remediation_summary: Enable MDVM via Defender for Servers.

title: Ensure Key Vault has soft delete and purge protection enabled
description: Verifies that every Key Vault has soft delete and purge protection turned on.
rationale: Prevents irreversible destruction of keys/secrets and supports recovery.
impact: Soft-deleted vaults consume quota for retention period.
audit_procedure: Azure Portal → Key Vault → Properties → Soft delete / Purge protection
remediation_summary: Set soft_delete_retention_days and purge_protection_enabled=true.

title: Ensure Key Vault has key rotation policy
description: Checks that keys stored in Key Vault have an auto-rotation policy configured.
rationale: Regular rotation reduces the window of exposure for compromised keys.
audit_procedure: Azure Portal → Key Vault → Keys → Rotation policy
remediation_summary: Define rotation_policy with automatic time-based rotation.

title: Ensure Key Vault diagnostic settings are configured
description: Verifies Key Vault sends AuditEvent logs to Log Analytics or storage.
rationale: Audit records are mandatory for detecting secret access patterns.
audit_procedure: Azure Portal → Key Vault → Diagnostic settings
remediation_summary: Create monitor_diagnostic_setting on KV with AuditEvent logs.

title: Ensure Key Vault uses private endpoint
description: Checks that Key Vault is accessed via Private Endpoint with public access disabled.
rationale: Keeps secret access on the Azure backbone and off the public internet.
audit_procedure: Azure Portal → Key Vault → Networking → Private endpoint
remediation_summary: Create private endpoint and set public_network_access_enabled=false.

title: Ensure Key Vault diagnostic logs are captured
description: Verifies that diagnostic logs include AllLogs or AuditEvent category for long-term retention.
rationale: Without captured logs, secret-access forensics is impossible.
audit_procedure: Azure Portal → Key Vault → Diagnostic settings → Logs → AuditEvent enabled
remediation_summary: Ensure diagnostic setting captures AuditEvent category.

title: Ensure App Service uses HTTPS only
description: Verifies App Service httpsOnly property is set to true.
rationale: Redirects all traffic to TLS and prevents credential leakage over HTTP.
audit_procedure: Azure Portal → App Service → TLS/SSL settings → HTTPS Only = On
remediation_summary: Set https_only=true on azurerm_linux_web_app / windows_web_app.

title: Ensure App Service minimum TLS version is 1.2
description: Checks that minTlsVersion is at least 1.2 on App Service.
rationale: Blocks deprecated TLS 1.0/1.1 that are vulnerable to BEAST/POODLE.
audit_procedure: Azure Portal → App Service → TLS/SSL settings → Minimum TLS Version = 1.2
remediation_summary: Set site_config.minimum_tls_version to 1.2.

title: Ensure App Service remote debugging is disabled
description: Verifies that remoteDebuggingEnabled is false.
rationale: Remote debug ports give full code execution if credentials leak.
impact: Developers must use staging slot for debug.
audit_procedure: Azure Portal → App Service → Configuration → General settings → Remote debugging = Off
remediation_summary: Set site_config.remote_debugging_enabled=false.

title: Ensure App Service HTTP/2 is enabled
description: Checks that http20Enabled is true on the App Service site.
rationale: HTTP/2 improves performance and enforces TLS on modern clients.
impact: Minimal risk; legacy clients fall back to HTTP/1.1.
audit_procedure: Azure Portal → App Service → Configuration → General settings → HTTP version = 2.0
remediation_summary: Set site_config.http2_enabled=true.

title: Ensure App Service has managed identity configured
description: Verifies the App Service has a system or user assigned managed identity.
rationale: Managed identities eliminate hard-coded credentials in app configuration.
audit_procedure: Azure Portal → App Service → Identity → System assigned = On
remediation_summary: Set identity block with type SystemAssigned.
